mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% while CPI inflation accelerates above 8.0% year-over-year, real rates turn deeply negative. Equity investors rationally shift capital away from bonds into stocks as the real return on fixed income becomes uncompetitive, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative in early 2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with lagged inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor markets and wage-driven inflation expectations. Equity investors interpret this as a signal that the Federal Reserve will maintain or accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% as of March 2022, approaching 4.0% threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with Fed tightening bias
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE remains below 4.0% for 3+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.2 million annualized units, signaling weakening consumer demand and forward-looking recession risk. Equity investors reduce risk exposure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND HOUST < 1.2 million annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1.142 million as of February 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both conditions are met, or Spearman rank correlation between the composite demand signal and forward returns < 0.08 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates to below 0.3% month-over-month, indicating supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. Equity investors reduce exposure to cyclical stocks, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with supply constraints
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO growth falls below 0.3% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) remains above 400,000 persons per month while the unemployment rate stays below 4.5%, indicating a persistently tight labor market. This combination signals sustained wage inflation and justifies continued Federal Reserve rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > 400000 AND UNRATE < 4.5% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144,747 thousand as of March 2022
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with Fed tightening bias
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of instances where both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2.0% annualized while the federal funds rate is below 1.0%, indicating monetary policy is accommodative relative to economic growth. This positive real growth signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in sustained economic expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16583.1 billion as of Q4 2021
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 8.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, signaling that real rates are deeply negative and the Fed is behind the inflation curve. Equity investors anticipate aggressive future rate hikes and repricing of equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 240.305 as of February 2022, implying ~8.6% YoY inflation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation surprise
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month while nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) exceeds 500,000 persons per month, signaling robust supply-side expansion and labor market strength. This dual supply-labor signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in sustained economic momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.4 and PAYEMS at 144,747 thousand as of February-March 2022
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong supply normalization
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any rolling 12-month window.
